helpful: Provisioned petition.txt and questions-presented.txt made the vehicle analysis possible without any fetching
Statpack relist-count and CVSG cuts gave a direct anchor for a zero-relist petition
The snapshot's ProceedingsandOrder entries (waiver of response, conference distribution) carried the strongest deny signals
gaps: The statpack referenced in the prompt as carrying a 'Segment base rate by salience band' table does not include that section in the committed version, so the salience-band anchor could not be used
A per-state-court cut with more Wisconsin rows would help; the originating-court table covers few state courts
A statpack cut keyed on 'response waived, no CFR' status would capture what is arguably the single strongest pre-conference deny signal
notes: Forward cell with a same-day snapshot; no MCP retrieval was needed. One corpus query sufficed to profile recent grants.
